# Log Link (Poisson GLM)

A **Poisson GLM** models count data (non-negative integers) using a log link function to connect the linear predictor to the Poisson mean.

## The model

$$\text{count}_i \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_i)$$

$$\log(\lambda_i) = \alpha + \beta \, x_i$$

The log link ensures that $\lambda_i > 0$ for all values of the linear predictor.

## Why a log link?

The Poisson distribution requires $\lambda > 0$. A direct linear model $\lambda_i = \alpha + \beta x_i$ could predict negative means. By modelling $\log(\lambda)$ instead, we guarantee positivity: $\lambda = e^{\alpha + \beta x}$ is always positive.

## Interpreting parameters

- **$\alpha$** (intercept): the log mean count when $x = 0$. On the original scale, the mean count is $e^\alpha$.
- **$\beta$** (slope): a one-unit increase in $x$ multiplies the expected count by $e^\beta$.

For example, if $\beta = 0.8$, then a one-unit increase in $x$ multiplies the expected count by $e^{0.8} \approx 2.2$.

## BUGS syntax

```
for (i in 1:N) {
  count[i] ~ dpois(lambda[i])
  log(lambda[i]) <- alpha + beta * x[i]
}
```

The link function appears on the **left** side of the deterministic assignment. FANGS evaluates $\lambda_i = \exp(\alpha + \beta x_i)$ internally.

## Posterior sampling

This model is non-conjugate (the log link breaks conjugacy between $\alpha$/$\beta$ and the Poisson likelihood), so FANGS uses **slice sampling** for $\alpha$ and $\beta$.
